question: By the time the construction team finishes the foundation, the architects _____ the detailed drawings already.
explanation: Future perfect tense shows completion before another future action (sequence of tenses).

question: The panel of experts _____ that the seismic data _____ reliable for the bridge design.
explanation: Collective noun 'panel' takes singular verb; 'data' treated as singular in this engineering context.

question: Neither the project manager nor the site engineers _____ satisfied with the initial safety report.
explanation: Verb agrees with nearer plural subject in neither...nor construction.

question: A number of critical issues _____ identified during the quality audit last week.
explanation: 'A number of' always takes a plural verb.

question: The number of successful IoE candidates _____ steadily rising every year.
If the measurements had been taken accurately yesterday, the analysis _____ correct today.
explanation: Mixed conditional: past perfect unreal condition affecting present result.

question: The committee _____ divided in opinion but each member _____ submitted individual feedback.
explanation: Singular when acting as a unit; pronoun agreement with 'each'.

question: Hardly _____ the examination started when the electricity failed.
explanation: Inverted past perfect with 'hardly...when' for sequence of events.

question: The dean along with all faculty members _____ attending the accreditation meeting tomorrow.
explanation: Singular verb with phrases like 'along with', 'together with'.

question: By the end of this semester, all students _____ their final year projects.
explanation: Future perfect for action completed before a future time.

question: All the laboratory equipment, including the new CNC machines, _____ properly maintained.
explanation: 'Equipment' is uncountable and takes singular verb.

question: The data collected from multiple sensors _____ being analyzed by the research team.
explanation: In modern technical usage, 'data' is often singular.

question: She informed us that she _____ the thesis if she _____ more time.
Not only the structural calculations but also the cost estimate _____ found inaccurate.
explanation: Verb agrees with nearer singular subject.

question: When the inspector arrives tomorrow, the workers _____ the site cleared already.
The jury _____ still deliberating the case, but their decision _____ crucial for the project.
explanation: Jury as a single unit takes singular verbs.

question: Scarcely _____ we reached the construction site when the heavy rain started.
Sixty percent of the total budget _____ already allocated for materials.
explanation: Singular verb for percentage of singular noun.

question: He promised that he _____ the prototype by Friday if everything _____ according to plan.
explanation: Reported first conditional becomes second conditional.

question: The series of load tests _____ completed successfully, yet the final results _____ pending.
The engineer said, 'The building will withstand the earthquake if reinforcements are added.'
options: The engineer said that the building would withstand the earthquake if reinforcements were added.
The engineer said that the building will withstand if reinforcements are added.
The engineer said the building would have withstood if reinforcements had been added.
The engineer said that the building withstands if reinforcements were added.
explanation: Proper backshift in reported conditional statement.

question: 'Finish the report before you leave,' the supervisor ordered the intern.
options: The supervisor ordered the intern to finish the report before he left.
The supervisor ordered finish the report before you leave.
The supervisor ordered that the intern finishes the report before leaving.
The supervisor ordered the intern to have finished the report before he leaves.
explanation: Imperative in reported speech uses 'to + infinitive' with tense adjustment.

question: The lecturer said, 'Steel expands when heated.'
options: The lecturer said that steel expanded when heated.
The lecturer said that steel expands when heated.
The lecturer said steel would expand when heated.
The lecturer said that steel has expanded when heated.

question: The students asked, 'When will the results be declared?'
options: The students asked when the results would be declared.
The students asked when the results will be declared.
The students asked when would the results be declared.
The students asked when the results are declared.
explanation: Wh-question in indirect speech with backshift and statement word order.

question: The data _____ shown that the new alloy _____ superior strength under stress.
explanation: Singular 'data' in this context; singular subject-verb agreement.

question: Every engineer and every architect _____ required to submit the safety compliance form.
One of the main reasons for the delay _____ the shortage of skilled labor.
If I _____ the entrance exam last year, I _____ studying engineering now.
explanation: Third conditional mixed with present result.

question: The government as well as the private firms _____ investing heavily in sustainable infrastructure.
No sooner _____ the lecture ended than the discussion began.
The pair of calipers _____ missing from the workshop.
She said that she _____ to the conference if she _____ invited.
Mathematics _____ my favorite subject, but the advanced topics _____ quite challenging.
explanation: Singular for subject name; plural for 'topics'.

question: The flock of birds _____ flying south when the storm hit.
By 2028, Nepal _____ significant progress in hydropower generation.
The news about the bridge collapse _____ shocking to everyone involved.
Either the blueprints or the calculations _____ to be revised immediately.
explanation: Verb agrees with nearer plural noun.

question: The professor explained that iron _____ when exposed to moisture.
explanation: Universal truth in reported speech - present tense retained.

question: 'Why did you miss the lab session?' the instructor asked.
options: The instructor asked why I had missed the lab session.
The instructor asked why did I miss the lab session.
The instructor asked why I missed the lab session.
The instructor asked why I have missed the lab session.

question: The statistics _____ misleading unless properly interpreted.
A variety of engineering solutions _____ proposed during the brainstorming session.
If the foundation had been stronger, the building _____ during the tremor.
The staff _____ unhappy with the new policy changes.
explanation: 'Staff' can take plural verb when referring to individuals.

question: None of the applicants _____ qualified for the scholarship this year.
explanation: 'None of' + plural noun takes plural verb.

question: The CEO announced that the company _____ new software by the next quarter.
explanation: Future tense in reported speech when reporting recent announcement.

question: Physics as well as Chemistry _____ compulsory for engineering students.
explanation: Singular verb for compound subjects joined by 'as well as'.

question: The cattle _____ grazing in the field near the construction site.
By the time we submit the report, the review committee _____ its decision.
The United Nations _____ an important role in global engineering standards.
'Please submit your assignments on time,' the teacher said.
options: The teacher requested to submit assignments on time.
The teacher asked us to submit our assignments on time.
The teacher said please submit your assignments on time.
The teacher ordered that assignments are submitted on time.

question: The information provided in the manual _____ very useful.
Many a student _____ failed to clear the IOE entrance due to poor time management.
If he _____ harder, he _____ selected for the merit list.
The headquarters of the company _____ located in Kathmandu.
The audience _____ applauding loudly after the presentation.
She asked me whether I _____ the new software by the deadline.
explanation: Reported yes/no question with conditional backshift.

question: The politics of infrastructure development _____ complex in Nepal.
explanation: 'Politics' singular when referring to the field.

question: Ten kilometers _____ a long distance to cover on foot for site inspection.
explanation: Distance as a single unit - singular verb.

question: The engineer claimed that the design _____ approved if minor changes _____ made.
The majority of engineers _____ in favor of the new regulation.
explanation: 'Majority of' + plural noun takes plural verb.

question: The team leader said, 'We have completed phase one.'
options: The team leader said that they have completed phase one.
The team leader said that they had completed phase one.
The team leader said we have completed phase one.
The team leader said they completed phase one.
explanation: Present perfect backshifts to past perfect.

question: Economics _____ taught as an elective in many engineering programs.
If the tender documents _____ ready by Monday, the bidding process _____ on time.
explanation: First conditional for real future possibility.

question: The premises of the institute _____ well guarded.
The jury _____ its verdict after long deliberation.
Not until the final inspection _____ completed will the project be handed over.
The furniture in the new lab _____ of high quality.
He asked me if I _____ the calculations before submitting the report.
explanation: Past perfect in reported question for earlier action.

question: A flock of errors _____ detected in the initial code review.
The Ministry of Education _____ new guidelines for technical education.
If I _____ you, I _____ the offer immediately.
explanation: Second conditional with 'were' for all subjects.

question: The contents of the report _____ confidential.
The police _____ investigating the case of equipment theft from the site.
